heading: Accessible trails
label: 8484 Accessible trails

children.text: on National Forest System lands in each region of the Forest Service;
on land managed by the National Park Service in each region of the National Park Service;
on land managed by the Bureau of Land Management in each region of the Bureau of Land Management; and
on land managed by the United States Fish and Wildlife Service in each region of the United States Fish and Wildlife Service.

children.label: B
children.text: modify an existing trail into an accessible trail; or

children.label: C
children.text: create an accessible trail from a combination of new and existing trails; and

children.label: A
children.text: consult with stakeholders with respect to the feasibility and resources necessary for completing the accessible trail;

children.label: B
children.text: ensure the accessible trail complies with the Architectural Barriers Act of 1968 ( et seq.) and ; and42 U.S.C. 4151section 794 of title 29

children.label: C
children.text: to the extent practicable, ensure that outdoor constructed features supporting the accessible trail, including trail bridges, parking spaces, and restroom facilities, meet the requirements of the Architectural Barriers Act of 1968 ( et seq.) and .42 U.S.C. 4151section 794 of title 29

children.heading: Completion
children.label: c Completion
children.text: Not later than 7 years after , the Secretary concerned, in coordination with stakeholders described under subsection (b)(2), shall complete each accessible trail developed under subsection (a).January 4, 2025

children.heading: Maps, signage, and promotional materials
children.label: d Maps, signage, and promotional materials

children.text: publish and distribute maps and install signage, consistent with Architectural Barriers Act of 1968 accessibility guidelines and ; andsection 794d of title 29
coordinate with stakeholders to leverage any non-Federal resources necessary for the development, stewardship, completion, or promotion of the accessible trail.

children.heading: Conflict avoidance with other uses
children.label: e Conflict avoidance with other uses
A
children.text: the uses in effect before , with respect to any trail that is part of that accessible trail;January 4, 2025

children.label: B
children.text: multiple-use areas where biking, hiking, horseback riding, off-highway vehicle recreation, or use by pack and saddle stock are existing uses on ; orJanuary 4, 2025

children.label: C
children.text: the purposes for which any trail is established under the National Trails System Act ( et seq.); and16 U.S.C. 1241
complies with all applicable land use and management plans of the Federal recreational lands and waters on which the accessible trail is located.

children.heading: Interim report
children.label: 1 Interim report
children.text: Not later than 3 years after , the Secretary concerned, in coordination with stakeholders and other interested organizations, shall prepare and publish an interim report that lists the accessible trails developed under this section during the previous 3 years.January 4, 2025

children.heading: Final report
children.label: 2 Final report
children.text: Not later than 7 years after , the Secretary concerned, in coordination with stakeholders and other interested organizations, shall prepare and publish a final report that lists the accessible trails developed under this section.January 4, 2025
